real.headline: Qatar Airways set to restart flights to Dubai, Sharjah, and Damascus
real.body:
Qatar Airways has announced the resumption of flight operations between Doha and key destinations across the Middle East, including the reinstatement of daily services to the United Arab Emirates and Syria.

Starting April 23, the airline will resume daily flights to Dubai International Airport (DXB) and Sharjah International Airport (SHJ). A daily service to Damascus International Airport (DAM) will follow from May 1.

Qatar Airways said these resumptions form part of a phased restoration of its global network, building on the carrier's mid‑April announcement confirming that its flight schedule will expand from June 16 to serve more than 150 destinations across six continents.

The airline has advised passengers to regularly check its official website or mobile app for the latest updates, and to ensure that their contact details are correct and up to date in their bookings so they can receive any notifications about schedule changes.

Qatar Airways has also outlined flexible options for customers with upcoming travel. If you have a confirmed booking with a travel date between 28 February and 15 September 2026, you are eligible for:

Qatar Airways stressed that all flight schedules remain subject to change or cancellation due to operational, regulatory, safety or other circumstances beyond its control, and urged customers to review the latest information before travelling.

real.headline: HH The Amir, Syrian President review regional and international developments
real.body:
HH The Amir Sheikh Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani met with Syrian President Ahmed Al Sharaa at the Amiri Diwan on Wednesday.

HH The Amir, at the beginning of the meeting, welcomed the Syrian President and his accompanying delegation, expressing his hope that this visit would contribute to strengthening cooperation between the two brotherly countries and elevating it to broader levels in various fields.

For his part, Al Sharaa expressed his appreciation to Sheikh Tamim for the warm welcome and generous hospitality, affirming his country's keenness to develop and enhance cooperation with Qatar in a way that serves the common interests of the two countries.

He also expressed his country's solidarity with Qatar following the Iranian attacks and several other countries in the region, and its support for the measures Qatar is taking to protect its sovereignty, security, and the safety of its citizens.

The two sides affirmed their commitment to supporting security and stability in the region. They also reviewed bilateral cooperation between the two countries and ways to strengthen and develop it across various fields.

The meeting was attended by Qatar's Prime Minister and Minister of Foreign Affairs HE Sheikh Mohammed bin Abdulrahman bin Jassim Al Thani, Chief of the Amiri Diwan HE Abdullah bin Mohammed Al Khulaifi, Minister of State at the Ministry of Foreign Affairs HE Dr. Mohammed bin Abdulaziz Al Khulaifi, and a number of other senior officials.

From the Syrian side, the meeting was attended by the Minister of Foreign Affairs and Expatriates Asaad Hassan Al Shaibani, and a number of other senior officials.

HH the Amir and His Excellency the Syrian President held a bilateral meeting, during which they exchanged views on key issues of mutual interest.

real.headline: Over 100 farms to take part in Local Dates Exhibition
real.body:
More than 100 local farms have confirmed their participation in the 9th Local Dates Exhibition, which will be held at the Souq Waqif from Tuesday (July 23) until August 3.

Organised by the Souq Waqif Management in cooperation with the Ministry of Municipality's Agricultural Affairs Department, the exhibition will take place at Souq Waqif's eastern square.

Souq Waqif Director Mohamed Abdullah Al Salem said all necessary arrangements were completed for the exhibition's hosting. The exhibition, which will be held inside an air-conditioned tent, will be open to the public from 4pm to 9pm every day. On Fridays, the festival will run from 4pm to 10pm.

"Souq Waqif is a prominent and important heritage and tourism destination for citizens, residents, and tourists. It's position in the heart of the city also makes it easy for everyone to reach here," Al Salem was quoted as saying by Gulf Times.

He said the exhibition's 9th edition will witness the participation of many local farms. All types of date products, as well as date syrups, will be available on sale.

"The exhibition has a great reputation. The previous edition saw record sales of 220 tonnes of dates. We expect the sales to be higher this time due to the high quality of Qatari dates," Al Salem said.

Agricultural Affairs Department Director Yousef Khaled Al Khulaifi said the exhibition aimed to promote date production in the country and also introduce its various types.

"It encourages local farm owners to display and market their dates directly to the public. It will increase their sales volume and enhance their ability to increase production and develop its marketing methods," Al Khulaifi said.

The exhibition's organising committee has set strict conditions for participation. The dates must be good, clean, and free of infection, insects, and impurities. A specialist team will collect samples of the wetlands of participating farms for examination and analysis to ensure they are free of toxic substances and traces of pesticides.

real.headline: MoPH announces Ramadan working hours for government health sector
real.body:
Qatar's Ministry of Public Health (MoPH) announced updated working hours for the government health sector during Ramadan, taking into account its special nature.

The MoPH stated that all emergency and inpatient services at Hamad Medical Corporation (HMC) hospitals, including paediatric Emergency Centers, will continue to operate as usual around the clock throughout the week.

HMC outpatient clinics and the urgent consultation service will operate via the number 16000 from Sunday to Thursday, from 8am until 1pm throughout Ramadan.

The Primary Health Care Corporation (PHCC) will also provide urgent care services at 14 health centers around the clock, seven days a week.

The MoPH stated that government healthcare institutions will announce the detailed working hours specific to their services.

The MoPH also announced the working hours for a number of its service departments during Ramadan, with the Medical Commission Department receiving visitors from 9am until 5pm.

The Department of Medical Relations and Treatment Abroad will receive visitors at the MoPH building from 9am to 1pm

Birth registration offices affiliated with the MoPH in government and private hospitals will operate in the morning from 9.30am to 1.30pm, while evening operations will be at the Birth Registration Office in the Women Wellness and Research Center from 1.30pm to 4.30pm.

The Births and Deaths Committee at the MoPH will receive visitors during the holy month from 9.30am to 1.30pm.

The Death Registration Unit will receive visitors at the Unified Office for Humanitarian Services from 9am to 5pm, and on Fridays and Saturdays from 9am to 2pm.

The Unified Healthcare Contact Center (16000) will operate around the clock, 7 days a week.

pilot.headline: MoPH Announces Ramadan Working Hours for Government Health Sector
pilot.body:
The Ministry of Public Health (MoPH) has announced updated working hours for the government health sector during the holy month of Ramadan, taking into account its special nature.

According to the MoPH, all emergency and inpatient services at Hamad Medical Corporation (HMC) hospitals, including paediatric Emergency Centers, will continue to operate as usual around the clock throughout the week. HMC outpatient clinics and the urgent consultation service will operate via the number 16000 from Sunday to Thursday, from 8am until 1pm throughout Ramadan.

The Primary Health Care Corporation (PHCC) will provide urgent care services at 14 health centers around the clock, seven days a week. The MoPH stated that government healthcare institutions will announce detailed working hours specific to their services.

The MoPH also announced working hours for several of its service departments. The Medical Commission Department will receive visitors from 9am until 5pm. The Department of Medical Relations and Treatment Abroad will receive visitors at the MoPH building from 9am to 1pm.

Birth registration offices affiliated with the MoPH in government and private hospitals will operate in the morning from 9.30am to 1.30pm, while evening operations will be at the Birth Registration Office in the Women Wellness and Research Center from 1.30pm to 4.30pm. The Births and Deaths Committee at the MoPH will receive visitors during the holy month from 9.30am to 1.30pm.

The Death Registration Unit will receive visitors at the Unified Office for Humanitarian Services from 9am to 5pm, and on Fridays and Saturdays from 9am to 2pm. The Unified Healthcare Contact Center (16000) will operate around the clock, 7 days a week.

real.headline: Ministry of Municipality launches eco-friendly fleet of vehicles
real.body:
The Ministry of Municipality has taken a significant stride towards cleaner, smarter urban services with the launch of a modern fleet of eco-friendly vehicles and equipment — a move that signals the country's deepening resolve to align its public infrastructure with global environmental benchmarks.

The fleet was inaugurated by Abdullah Ahmed al-Karani, assistant undersecretary for Public Services Affairs, who described the initiative as a clear expression of the ministry's commitment to embedding best environmental practices into its day-to-day operations. He said the new vehicles would sharpen the efficiency of field operations while making a tangible contribution to improving air quality nationwide.

Sherida Sultan al-Rumaihi, director of the Mechanical Equipment Department, called the rollout a qualitative leap in operational capability, noting that every vehicle in the fleet had been selected against the most rigorous technical and environmental criteria, in step with the latest advances in global technology and in full alignment with sustainable development goals.

At the heart of the fleet's green credentials are Euro 6 engines — among the most demanding emissions standards in the world — which significantly cut harmful exhaust emissions, boost fuel efficiency, and reduce noise pollution. Safety, too, has been woven into the fleet's design, with advanced warning lights and high-visibility reflective elements fitted across the vehicles.

The fleet itself is as diverse as the municipal challenges it is built to meet. From hearse transport ambulances and refrigerated vans to street washing vehicles, waste collection trucks, and container carriers, the range covers a broad operational spectrum. Rounding out the lineup are a street sweeper, a beach cleaning tractor, and a dedicated waste collection vehicle — together equipping the ministry to serve Qatar's growing urban landscape with greater precision, sustainability, and care.

real.headline: Qatar condemns Iranian attacks targeting Turkiye, Azerbaijan
real.body:
Qatar has strongly condemned attempts to target Turkiye with a ballistic missile and an airport in Azerbaijan using Iranian drones.

In a statement on Thursday, Qatar's Ministry of Foreign Affairs described the incidents as a 'dangerous escalation' and a 'blatant violation of state sovereignty,' warning that they threaten regional security and stability. The ministry said the attacks were part of a series of 'reckless' actions by Iran.

It added that Iran's continued opening of new fronts and expansion of tensions with neighboring countries represents a serious risk to the stability of the region.

Qatar called on Iran to immediately stop 'irresponsible policies that undermine regional security,' advising it to prioritize the interests of the region's people and adhere to the principles of good neighbourliness and international law.

The statement also reaffirmed Qatar's full solidarity with Turkiye and Azerbaijan, and its support for measures taken by both countries to safeguard their sovereignty, security, and territorial integrity.

pilot.headline: Qatar Condemns Iranian Attacks on Turkiye and Azerbaijan
pilot.body:
